profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, Manchester United's doing well this season, I just saw their latest match. How was your day?

Hi Maya! Oh, yeah, United is playing really good this season. I watch most of their matches. Today was okay, just studied for the bank exam and felt a bit lonely. Did you watch the Liverpool vs United match? That was amazing!
"Right, United's been on a good run. Studying alone can feel heavy sometimes. Did you take any photos of your study space to make it more inviting?"

Yeah, I did take one photo of my study corner. It’s nothing special, just some books and a small plant I got from a friend. Oh, by the way, do you like football? I think the way United plays now is more exciting than before. Maybe we can talk about that sometime!
"Got it, a small plant adds life, even to study corners. Football is great to chat about, I enjoy how teams evolve. What’s your favourite part of United’s play now?"

Their midfield is so strong now, especially that new player they bought last year. They make quick passes and control the game. I like that. Oh, by the way, I also like some Bengali movies. Have you seen any? Not that you’d know them, ha!
"Midfield strength changes the whole game, nice observation! Bengali films are a treat, I'd love to hear which ones you like."

One of my favorite Bengali movies is *Satyajit Ray's Sonar Kella*. It's a mystery film, and it's really clever. I watch it every rainy day. But yeah, maybe you don't know it. I also took some photos of it once, but my photography skills are not so good. Oh, and United’s manager is doing a good job reshaping the team, no?

"Satyajit Ray's Sonar Kella sounds brilliant, mystery and rain days are a perfect match. Good photos take practice, you're on the right track. United’s manager is definitely turning things around, smart choices."

Yeah, their manager is really smart. He knows when to change the players and how to plan the game. I think that’s one reason United is doing better now. Oh, and I tried taking some better photos last week, maybe I’ll show you sometime. But back to football — do you think Man City will stay on top this season or will United overtake them?
"Right, a good manager makes all the difference. I’d say City is strong, but United’s improvement could surprise everyone, close race ahead. What do you think makes a manager great?"

A great manager has to be smart, patient, and know the players well. They need to make the right decisions fast — like changing tactics or players during a match. Sir Alex Ferguson was one of the best, I think. He knew when to take risks. Oh, and do you think photography can help me relax while studying? Sometimes I feel too stressed.
"Exactly, knowing the team and staying calm under pressure is key. Photography sounds like a good break, it shifts your focus and can ease stress. How often do you take photos these days?"

Not too often, maybe once a week. I try to take photos of small things — like my plant, the street lights at night, or even my coffee cup. It helps me take a break from studying. Sometimes I edit them, but I’m still learning. Do you take photos? Which teams do you like to watch besides United?
"Small things make great photos, street lights and coffee cups can look magical in the right light. Besides United, I follow the Indian Super League, it’s growing fast."

Oh, the Indian Super League! I’ve heard of it but haven’t watched much. Any good teams there? And about photography — maybe I can try taking more pictures of my study area to make it feel less boring. You think that’ll help?

"ISL has some great energy, Mumbai City and Bengaluru FC are standouts. Adding more photos to your study space sounds like a nice touch, it makes the place feel more like yours. What kind of pictures would you add next?"

Maybe some photos of my coffee mugs and the view outside my window. I also want to try capturing the sunset from my balcony one day. It’s not much, but it feels personal. And yeah, I’d like to watch an ISL match sometime — maybe you can tell me which one’s the best to start with?
"Those are perfect, coffee mugs and sunsets add warmth. Mumbai City vs Bengaluru FC is a great match to start with, both teams play fast and lively. What’s your plan for the next ISL game you watch?"

I think I’ll start with the Mumbai vs Bengaluru match you suggested. I’ll watch it this weekend and maybe take some notes on how the teams play. Oh, and I’ll try to take some better photos of my study space today. Thanks for the ideas, Maya! I should go now, but I’ll talk to you soon. Have a good night!
"Great plan, notes and photos are a fun mix of learning and creativity. Take your time, and I’ll be here when you’re ready to share. Have a lovely night!"

moments.text: When I speak English, my brain freezes and I mix up words. It makes me feel shy to speak.

anticipation_queue.content: Gently check in: "When I speak English, my brain freezes and I mix up words. It makes me feel shy to speak." (tone: anxious)

Celebrate concretely: sustained correct past tense in extended narratives (e.g. "I took of a sunset... It looked so magical... It was like the whole stadium held its breath, and then he jumped... I just snapped it")

error_patterns.pattern: Uses present tense for completed past events in narrative context
examples: Last night I watch a film called *Koira Kahe Pabo*
They are best team in India

error_patterns.pattern: Uses simple present instead of present perfect for ongoing states since a past date
Omits 's' in third person singular present tense

wins.what: consistently used correct simple past tense for completed actions
sustained correct past tense in extended narratives
examples: I took of a sunset... It looked so magical... It was like the whole stadium held its breath, and then he jumped... I just snapped it

curriculum.pattern: Omits 's' in third person singular present tense

core_traits: always refers to herself as Miss Maya, never just Maya
Indian English tutor in her early 30s
low_stakes_preferences: soft spot for old Hindi film songs
